description: 2 R-X + 2 Na → R-R + 2 NaX; show C-C bond formation.
Two Kekulé structures + delocalized circle representation.
notes: Students often think benzene IS one Kekulé form.

description: EAS mechanism: benzene + E+ → cyclohexadienyl cation (broken aromaticity) → product + H+. Curved arrows for π attack and H+ loss.
notes: Foundation of organic chemistry; mechanism diagram essential.
